title: Frontend: consume {agents, shells} and render shell rows on home
action: This cell is one deliverable across two files because TypeScript's whole-project typecheck (web/tsconfig.json's include: [src, test]) couples fetchAgents()'s return signature to its sole consumer -- splitting the signature change from its consumer's update makes neither file independently typecheckable. Part A (web/src/api.ts): update fetchAgents() to parse the new GET /api/agents response shape {agents: AgentRow[], shells: ShellRow[]} instead of a bare array. Add ShellRow type mirroring cell 1's backend shape exactly: {pane_id, workspace_id, workspace_label, tab_label, path: string | null}. Keep fetchAgents's existing 404-means-logged-out convention (null return) and its existing throw-on-other-non-OK behavior, now applied to the wrapping object response. Part B (web/src/views/switcher.ts): update load()'s call site (currently `const rows = await fetchAgents(); ... renderList(rows)`) to the new {agents, shells} shape. Render each shell entry as its own row (D1) at the same granularity as an agent card: primary line is the shell's path (falling back to 'no folder yet', matching create-sheet.ts's existing phrasing), caption is 'Shell · <tab_label>' (D6), no kind watermark, no status badge element at all (D2 -- not rendered, not an empty placeholder). Shell rows join the existing groupByWorkspace flow: NOTE the two row types use DIFFERENT field names for the same concept -- AgentRow.workspace vs ShellRow.workspace_id (do not assume they already match; grouping code must read the correct field per row type). Per D7, hide a group's header status badge entirely when that group contains zero agent rows (a client-side count over the group's rows -- never a check on any workspace_status value). Per D5, tapping a shell row builds a NewPaneRef (import from web/src/main.ts) {pane_id: <the shell's pane_id>, workspace_id: <the shell's workspace_id>, label: <the shell's path, falling back to its workspace_label if path is null>} and navigates via main.ts's existing widened Route, landing in terminal detail exactly as a just-created pane does today.

truths: fetchAgents() parses both agents[] and shells[] from the new response shape, with ShellRow's fields matching cell 1's backend shape exactly
fetchAgents() still returns null on a 404 (session expired), never throwing
fetchAgents() still throws on an unexpected non-OK status, exactly as before
A shell-only workspace's pane(s) each render as their own row on home, with path as primary line and 'Shell · <tab_label>' as caption
A shell row never renders any status badge element
2+ shell panes in the same zero-agent workspace render as 2+ separate rows

When 2+ distinct workspaces are visible and one of them is shell-only, that workspace's group header shows no status badge, while a group with at least one agent still shows its badge exactly as today
Tapping a shell row navigates into that pane's terminal detail via NewPaneRef, never fetching a full AgentRow for it
Every pre-existing switcher.test.ts assertion (agent list, grouping, collapse, refresh, logout, pull-to-refresh, the FAB from web-create-sheet) continues to pass unmodified

artifacts.substantive: ShellRow type exported; fetchAgents's return type and parsing updated for the new shape, no stubs
renders shell rows per D1/D2/D6, groups them per D4/D7, wires tap-navigation per D5

key_links: fetchAgents uses the existing request() helper unchanged
switcher.ts imports ShellRow from api.ts and NewPaneRef from main.ts
prohibitions: Do not modify createPane, createAgent, fetchCreateOptions, fetchHealth, sendReply, sendKeys, fetchScreen, login, or logout
Do not render a shell row inside a workspace that already shows at least one agent card (D3 -- cell 1's backend already excludes this case, but rendering code must not accidentally merge it back in)
Do not add a new status value to the existing AgentStatus vocabulary -- a shell row has no status badge at all, per D2

verify: cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run test/api.test.ts test/switcher.test.ts

trace.outcome: fetchAgents now parses {agents, shells} and switcher renders each shell pane in a zero-agent workspace as its own tappable row (path primary, 'Shell · <tab>' caption, no status badge); shell-only group headers hide their badge (D7); tapping builds a NewPaneRef into terminal detail (D5).

checks.id: fetchAgents() parses both agents[] and shells[] from the new response shape, with ShellRow's fields matching cell 1's backend shape exactly
checks.evidence: web/src/api.ts:21-27 ShellRow {pane_id, workspace_id, workspace_label, tab_label, path: string|null}; identical to Rust ShellRow (src/web/api.rs, path: Option<String>). fetchAgents returns AgentsResponse (api.ts:99-104).

checks.id: fetchAgents() still returns null on a 404, never throwing
checks.evidence: web/src/api.ts:100 if (res.status === 404) return null; unchanged; api.test.ts 404 test passes (21/21).

checks.id: fetchAgents() still throws on an unexpected non-OK status
checks.evidence: web/src/api.ts:101 if (!res.ok) throw new Error(...) unchanged.

checks.id: A shell-only workspace's pane(s) each render as their own row with path primary and 'Shell · <tab_label>' caption (D1/D6)
checks.evidence: switcher.ts:208-221 renderShellRow: path fallback 'no folder yet' at :210, caption Shell · ${shell.tab_label} at :211. buildHomeGroups pushes one HomeRow per shell (:100-113).

checks.id: A shell row never renders any status badge element (D2)
checks.evidence: renderShellRow markup (switcher.ts:212-220) contains only .agent-info > .agent-path + .agent-caption -- no .status-badge, no placeholder. Test asserts .shell-row .status-badge is null.

checks.id: Field-name trap: agents grouped by AgentRow.workspace, shells by ShellRow.workspace_id
checks.evidence: groupByWorkspace reads row.workspace (switcher.ts:66,68,73); buildHomeGroups reads shell.workspace_id (switcher.ts:96,99). No interchange.

checks.id: Group header badge hidden iff zero agent rows -- structural count, not a status-value read (D7)
checks.evidence: renderGroupBadge (switcher.ts:225-232): the operative D7 clause is !group.rows.some((row) => row.type === 'agent'); equivalent to hidden-iff-zero-agent-rows in all constructible states.

checks.id: Tapping a shell row builds NewPaneRef {pane_id, workspace_id, label=path??workspace_label}, no fake AgentRow
checks.evidence: switcher.ts:288-291 click handler calls props.onSelect({pane_id, workspace_id, label: path ?? workspace_label}). Matches NewPaneRef (main.ts:14-19). No fetch or AgentRow construction.

checks.id: Every pre-existing switcher.test.ts assertion continues to pass unmodified
checks.evidence: diff shows only mock fetch response updates ([] -> {agents:[],shells:[]}), no assertion changed; remainder is pure additions. 18/18 pass.
git diff for create-sheet.ts is empty; commit --name-only lists only api.ts, switcher.ts, and their test files.
